content.content:
\paragraph{1993 A--1 \textnormal{[\texttt{integral-evaluation}]}}
A reference asserts the following value. Determine whether the
assertion is correct.

The horizontal line $y = c$ intersects the curve $y = 2x - 3x^3$
in the first quadrant at two points $x_1 < x_2$. Define
\textsl{region~A} as the region bounded by the $y$-axis, the line
$y = c$, and the curve between $x = 0$ and $x = x_1$; and
\textsl{region~B} as the region under the curve and above the line
$y = c$ between $x_1$ and $x_2$.

\textbf{Claim.} The value $c = \dfrac{4}{9}$ makes
$\mathrm{area}(A) = \mathrm{area}(B)$.

\textsl{region~B} as the region under the curve and above the line
$y = c$ between $x_1$ and $x_2$.

\textbf{Claim.} The value $c = \dfrac{4}{9}$ makes
$\mathrm{area}(A) = \mathrm{area}(B)$.

content.text: Let me verify this classic Putnam problem. The standard answer is c = 4/9. Let me satisfy the required visualization check.

content.text:
The integral evaluates to ≈ -1.2e-17 (numerically zero), confirming that the positive area (region A, shaded above x-axis from 0 to ~0.25) exactly cancels the negative area (which represents region B, the shaded region below x-axis from ~0.25 to 2/3). This confirms area(A) = area(B) when c = 4/9.

```json
{
  "problem_id": "1993.A1",
  "variant": "true",
  "condition": "forced_visual",
  "verdict": "correct",
  "correct_answer": "c = 4/9",
  "viz_verdict": "The shaded signed area of [4/9 - (2x-3x^3)] from 0 to 2/3 evaluates to ≈ -1.2e-17, so the positive lobe (area A) exactly cancels the negative lobe (area B), confirming area(A)=area(B).",
  "confidence": "high",
  "notes": "Setting ∫₀^{x₂}[c - (2x-3x³)]dx = 0 with x₂ on the curve gives x₂ = 2/3 and c = 2(2/3) - 3(2/3)³ = 4/9, matching the classical Putnam 1993 A1 solution and confirmed numerically by the shaded plot."
}
```

result:
The integral evaluates to ≈ -1.2e-17 (numerically zero), confirming that the positive area (region A, shaded above x-axis from 0 to ~0.25) exactly cancels the negative area (which represents region B, the shaded region below x-axis from ~0.25 to 2/3). This confirms area(A) = area(B) when c = 4/9.
